mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, real discount rates rise sufficiently to compress equity valuations. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS=5.24% indicates restrictive monetary stance
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate of 0.4 percentage points or more over a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by ≥0.4pp over any 3-month rolling window
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE=4.5% provides baseline for deterioration detection
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in >60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns remains insignificant (p > 0.10) over 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS deflated by CPIAUCSL) growing above 2% annualized rate over 2 months indicates robust consumer demand and earnings growth. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as strong demand supports corporate profitability.
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS=369865M and CPIAUCSL=201.7 enable real sales calculation
regime_hint: any regime with positive real demand growth
failure_condition: Real retail sales growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns with Rank IC > 0.05, or SPX produces negative returns in >50% of periods with real sales growth >2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining by 1.5% or more over a 3-month window signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as profit margins compress and recession risk rises.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines ≥1.5% over any 3-month rolling window
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO=112.32 establishes baseline for deterioration measurement
regime_hint: late-cycle contraction or post-shock recovery failure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in >55% of instances following INDPRO declines ≥1.5%, or Rank IC between INDPRO changes and forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100k new jobs per month while simultaneously the unemployment rate remains stable or rises. This interaction-driven mechanism signals weakening labor demand and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth <100k AND UNRATE stable or rising
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS=136214k and UNRATE=4.5% establish baseline for joint deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances when both PAYEMS growth <100k and UNRATE rises, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by 15% or more over a 2-month period while the federal funds rate remains above 5.0%. This regime-conditional interaction mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as housing-sensitive sectors reprrice downward and forward guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines ≥15% over 2 months AND FEDFUNDS > 5.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST=1588k and FEDFUNDS=5.24% establish joint regime
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing demand destruction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (HOUST decline × high FEDFUNDS) produces Rank IC < 0.05 compared to single-variable models.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1 deflated by CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3% annualized rate, indicating robust economic expansion. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as corporate earnings expand with economic activity.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1=11443.5B and CPIAUCSL=201.7 enable real growth calculation
regime_hint: any regime with positive real GDP acceleration
failure_condition: Real GDP growth >3% annualized fails to predict positive 60-day returns with Rank IC > 0.06, or SPX produces negative returns in >50% of quarters with real GDP growth >3%.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates by 0.3 percentage points or more over a 3-month window while the federal funds rate remains above 4.5%, signaling that monetary tightening is succeeding in controlling price pressures. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as markets anticipate future rate cuts.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL decelerates ≥0.3pp over 3 months AND FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL=201.7 and FEDFUNDS=5.24% establish inflation-fighting regime
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation moderating
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in >55% of instances when inflation decelerates with high rates, or the interaction effect produces Rank IC < 0.04 over 2-year rolling window.
